name: Shrimp in Tamarind Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. To make tamarind liquid: Pour boiling water over five or six fresh, peeled tamarind pods and let sit for 30 minutes. Strain the pulp through a fine mesh into a bowl. Shell the shrimp.
2. Toss the shelled shrimp in cornstarch and sesame oil, then refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
3. Combine the tamarind liquid, salt, fish sauce, sugar, and lemon juice in a bowl.
4. In another bowl, combine ginger, chili peppers, and shallots.
5. Heat oil in a skillet or wok until hot but not smoking. Add shrimp quickly and stir to separate them. Remove when just pink and drain on a wire rack.
6. Reduce oil in the skillet to two tablespoons, then heat over high heat. Add the ginger/chili mixture and stir until fragrant.
7. Return the tamarind sauce to the pan and bring to a boil. Add shrimp and stir for 1 minute or until reduced. Serve garnished with coriander leaves.
